children.heading: Establishment of House Services Revolving Fund
children.label: a Establishment of House Services Revolving Fund

children.text: The operation of the House Barber Shop.
The operation of the House Beauty Shop.
The operation of the House Restaurant System (including vending operations).
The provision of mail services to entities which are not part of the House of Representatives.
The payment of fees for the use of the exercise facility described in section 103(a).1See References in Text note below.1
The collection of promotional rebates and incentives on credit card purchases, balances, and payments.
The collection of a service fee from vendors of the Master Web Services Agreement or the Technology Services Contract for failure to abide by and maintain House of Representatives security policies.
The operation of the House Dry Cleaning and Laundry Service.
Other activities related to the operation of services offered by the House of Representatives, as approved by the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Use of amounts in Fund
children.label: b Use of amounts in Fund
children.text: Amounts in the Revolving Funds shall be used for any purpose designated by the Chief Administrative Officer, including purposes relating to energy and water conservation and environmental activities carried out in buildings, facilities, and grounds under the Chief Administrative Officer’s jurisdiction, upon notification provided by the Chief Administrative Officer to the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.2So in original. Probably should be “Fund”.2

children.heading: Transfer authority
children.label: c Transfer authority
children.text: The Revolving Fund shall be treated as a category of allowances and expenses for purposes of .section 5507(a) of this title

children.heading: Termination and transfer of existing funds and accounts
children.label: d Termination and transfer of existing funds and accounts

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each fund and account specified in paragraph (2) is hereby terminated, and the balance of each such fund and account is hereby transferred to the Revolving Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: The revolving fund for the House Barber Shop, established by the paragraph under the heading “HOUSE BARBER SHOPS REVOLVING FUND” in the matter relating to the House of Representatives in chapter III of title I of the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 1975 (; ).Public Law 93–55488 Stat. 1776

children.label: B
children.text: The revolving funds for the House Beauty Shop, established by the matter under the heading “” in the matter relating to administrative provisions for the House of Representatives in the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1970 (; ).house beauty shopPublic Law 91–14583 Stat. 347

children.label: C
children.text: The special deposit account established for the House of Representatives Restaurant by section 208 of the First Supplemental Civil Functions Appropriation Act, 1941, or any successor fund or account established for the receipt of revenues of the House Restaurant System.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect , and shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2005 and each succeeding fiscal year.October 1, 2004
